intent: A real-time, AI-driven project management ecosystem designed to bridge the gap between high-level strategic roadmaps and granular task execution. The platform uniquely utilizes predictive analytics to forecast potential project bottlenecks based on historical team velocity and resource allocation, automatically suggesting re-prioritization strategies to maintain critical path alignment.

entities.name: ProjectRoadmap
entities.description: The high-level strategic container defining milestones, OKRs, and long-term delivery timelines.

entities.name: ExecutionTask
entities.description: The granular unit of work containing status, assignee, effort estimates, and dependency linkages.

entities.name: VelocityModel
entities.description: A temporal data structure storing historical team performance metrics used to train predictive bottleneck forecasting.

entities.name: ResourceAllocation
entities.description: An entity tracking current team capacity, skill availability, and cross-project utilization levels.

entities.name: AnomalyDetector
entities.description: A stream-processing component that evaluates incoming status updates against historical norms to flag risks to the critical path.

entities.name: IntegrationAdapter
entities.description: An abstraction layer managing bidirectional state synchronization with external communication tools like Slack and MS Teams.

entities.name: OptimizationPolicy
entities.description: A rule-based set of constraints that guides the AI in generating re-prioritization strategies for tasks and resources.

architecture.description: The architecture follows an event-driven microservices pattern. ExecutionTasks stream updates via WebSockets to an Event Mesh (Kafka) to meet sub-200ms latency requirements. The AnomalyDetector consumes these event streams, correlating them with the VelocityModel to identify deviations from the ProjectRoadmap. When a bottleneck is detected, the system triggers the OptimizationPolicy engine, which interacts with the ResourceAllocation service to formulate mitigation strategies. These strategies are then pushed to the IntegrationAdapter, which orchestrates bidirectional communication with external channels using OAuth2-secured tokens. Local offline state transitions are cached in a client-side indexedDB and merged via idempotent event ingestion once connectivity is restored, ensuring eventual consistency without violating the source-of-truth Integrity maintained in the core database.

relationships: IntegrationAdapter tracks external service synchronization state

endpoints.summary: Get performance metrics for a specific team
Retrieve detected risks for a specific task
Trigger manual synchronization with an external service

rules.description: Ensure task dependencies do not create circular references during creation or update.
rules.logic: Perform a Directed Acyclic Graph traversal on the dependency tree of ExecutionTasks; if a new edge creates a cycle, reject the state update.

rules.description: Validate resource availability before finalizing a task assignment.
rules.logic: Calculate the sum of EffortEstimate for all assigned tasks in the target timeframe; if sum exceeds ResourceAllocation capacity, flag as over-allocated.

rules.description: Maintain idempotent synchronization for offline-to-online state transitions.
rules.logic: Compare the sequence ID and timestamp of the cached event from IndexedDB with the existing record in the database; apply only if sequence ID is greater than current.

rules.description: Trigger risk mitigation when status latency deviates from velocity norms.
rules.logic: If AnomalyDetector identifies that current task completion rate is less than 80% of the historical VelocityModel average for the specific task type, invoke OptimizationPolicy.

rules.description: Ensure OAuth2 token validity for all external integration communications.
rules.logic: Verify token expiration timestamp against system time; if expired, initiate refresh flow before executing API call via IntegrationAdapter.

rules.description: Enforce ProjectRoadmap milestone alignment for task scheduling.
rules.logic: Validate that the end date of an ExecutionTask does not exceed the target end date of its associated ProjectRoadmap milestone.
